---
title: "Chapter 19: Semi-Structured Interviewing"
description: "Chapter-level study notes for Handbook of Practical Program Evaluation."
book_id: "handbook-practical-program-evaluation"
collection_id: "evaluation-texts"
note_type: "Chapter"
chapter_number: 19
source_pages: "539-546"
citation_key: "newcomer_hatry_wholey_2015_handbook_practical_program_evaluation"
evidence_ids:
  - "chunk:handbook-practical-program-evaluation:ch19-chapter-19-semi-structured-interviewing:c0001"
  - "chunk:handbook-practical-program-evaluation:part-2:c0001"
source_status: "needs_review"
---

# Chapter 19: Semi-Structured Interviewing

<div class="chapter-meta"><span>539-546</span><span>Part 2: Practical Data Collection Procedures</span><span>Source status: needs_review</span></div>

## Core Claim

Semi-structured interviews use guides and probes to collect comparable but open-ended evidence.

## Summary

The chapter focuses on person-to-person interviewing with open-ended questions.

It covers arranging interviews, preparing guides, interviewing techniques, and analysis/reporting of interview results.

Semi-structured interviewing is especially important for field studies and case studies where evaluators need depth, explanation, and stakeholder interpretation.

## Key Concepts

- **semi-structured interview**: Interviewing that uses a guide while allowing probing and open-ended response.
- **fieldwork**: On-site or direct data collection used to understand implementation, context, and outcomes.
- **case study**: A design for describing or explaining program operation, context, and outcomes in bounded cases.

## Practice Implications

- Use interview guides to connect conversation to evaluation questions.
- Train interviewers in probing and listening.
- Plan analysis before collecting large volumes of interview data.

## Source Records

- `chunk:handbook-practical-program-evaluation:ch19-chapter-19-semi-structured-interviewing:c0001`
- `chunk:handbook-practical-program-evaluation:part-2:c0001`

## References

These notes summarise source-linked records from *Handbook of Practical Program Evaluation* [@newcomer_hatry_wholey_2015_handbook_practical_program_evaluation].
